mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation by lowering the discount rate and improving risk appetite, so a sustained decline in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign relation reverses in rolling 2-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% and inflation remains elevated, higher financing costs and tighter discounting pressure equity multiples, so the next 20 trading days should skew negative for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day forward returns more often than baseline, or if positive returns dominate despite high rates and elevated inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and slower earnings expectations, which should reduce equity demand and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal has no edge relative to unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer demand and supports revenue expectations, so positive sales momentum should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce above-baseline 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the relation is consistently absent in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with improving HOUST indicates broad supply-side expansion and easing real activity constraints, which should support risk assets and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in INDPRO and HOUST do not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or if the combined signal loses sign consistency.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior housing boom signals an imminent construction slowdown and weaker future demand, which should pressure cyclical equities and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-downturn triggers are not followed by below-baseline 60-day returns, or if the effect disappears after accounting for recent market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is slowing while PAYEMS growth also decelerates, the economy is losing both output and labor momentum, and equity earnings expectations should weaken enough to produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous deceleration in GDP and payrolls does not coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if either proxy alone fully explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market, a low UNRATE with continued PAYEMS gains supports wage income and consumer spending, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; the signal is strongest only while unemployment remains near cycle lows.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.8% and PAYEMS 3-month average growth remains positive
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment and payroll-growth triggers do not deliver positive 60-day returns above baseline, or if the effect disappears once UNRATE rises above 5.0%.
